channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South African Youth Drive Innovation and Community Impact Through Mandela Month Projects
item.description: This Mandela Month, South African high school learners are showcasing remarkable initiatives that address pressing community issues. A group of students built a cart to help elderly individuals transp...

item.title: Vietnam and Australian Clubs Participate in Shakey’s Collegiate Tournament
item.description: The article reports that Vietnam and Australian-based sports clubs have officially joined the Shakey’s Collegiate National Invitationals, a prominent university-level volleyball tournament in the Phil...

item.title: Mistrial in Stronach's sexual assault case and criticism of Nenshi's apology for controversial photo
item.description: The article discusses two major developments. First, a mistrial was declared in the sexual assault conviction of Frank Stronach, a prominent businessman, due to procedural irregularities. The complain...

item.title: Lasaco Assurance Recovers from Half-Year Loss Through Cost Efficiency
item.description: Lasaco Assurance has managed to reverse its financial fortunes after reporting a significant improvement in profitability. The company's recent performance shows a shift from a previous net loss of N7...

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau Penalised for Improving Lie at The Open as Herbert and Burns Set Major Records
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau faced a two-shot penalty at The Open after officials ruled he inadvertently improved his lie by stepping on deep grass around his ball on the fifth hole. The American, who finished h...

item.title: Tanzania's Youth Protests and the Role of Generation Z in Political Change
item.description: Tanzania’s Ambassador to Belgium, Luxembourg, and the EU, Abdallah Sefue, has addressed youth-led protests demanding political and social accountability. The demonstrations, driven by Generation Z, hi...

item.title: Gauhati High Court clarifies breast groping isn't part of routine gynaecological exams
item.description: The Gauhati High Court recently ruled that groping a minor patient's breasts during a gynaecological examination is not considered a routine medical procedure. The court upheld the conviction of Dr. A...

item.title: Cultural Diplomacy: Bayeux Tapestry Arrives in UK as Symbol of Post-Brexit Relations
item.description: The Bayeux tapestry, a historic embroidery depicting the Norman Conquest of England, has arrived at the British Museum after years of negotiations between the UK and France. This cultural exchange mar...

item.title: South Africa's Constitutional Challenge on Medical Assistance in Dying
item.description: The article discusses a constitutional challenge launched by DignitySA to overturn South Africa's blanket common-law prohibition on medical assistance in dying. The case, brought before the Gauteng Hi...

item.title: Norway's 8bn Grant Supports Tanzania's Clean Energy Initiatives
item.description: The article highlights Norway's significant financial contribution to Tanzania's clean energy projects, aiming to boost sustainable development. It also touches on other regional news, including healt...

item.title: Why Argentina's Unconventional Style Could Secure Their World Cup Victory
item.description: The article humorously argues that Argentina will win the FIFA World Cup final against Spain due to their chaotic, emotionally charged style of play and cultural identity, contrasting it with Spain's ...

item.title: Court rejects PDP leadership dispute case involving Wabara and Turaki
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has dismissed a legal action filed by ex-Senate President Godswill Akpakpokpo (Wabara) and other former officials against the Independent National Electoral Commission ...

item.title: 1970s Fashion: Bold Styles and Cultural Revolution in Youth Culture
item.description: These vintage photographs offer a vivid glimpse into the dynamic and expressive fashion trends of the 1970s, highlighting how young people embraced bold, colorful, and liberated styles as a form of se...

item.title: Two children from northern Alberta still missing, mother and partner sought for abduction
item.description: A six-year-old boy and his four-year-old half-brother remain missing in northern Alberta after their biological mother and her partner allegedly abducted them. RCMP confirmed the Amber Alert issued on...

item.title: Frigoni commends players' dedication to national volleyball team
item.description: Coach Angiolino Frigoni expressed pride in the commitment of volleyball players who participated in the national team's training sessions, emphasizing their dedication to representing the country. He ...

item.title: Nine kidnapped victims escape after terrorists fall asleep in Katsina forest
item.description: At least nine kidnapped victims escaped from their abductors during a military operation in Katsina State. The Joint Task Force North West, Operation Fansan Yamma, rescued the victims and recovered 56...

item.title: Taylor Farms links cyclospora outbreak to lettuce supply chain
item.description: A significant foodborne illness outbreak linked to cyclosporiasis has been traced to shredded iceberg lettuce sourced from Taylor Farms' facility in Guanajuato, Mexico. Health officials in Michigan ha...

item.title: The Failed Backlash Against Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's 'The Odyssey' has defied a significant backlash, achieving record-breaking box office success despite intense online criticism. The film, which faced accusati...

item.title: Escalating U.S.-Iran Conflict Raises Fears of Larger War
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are engaged in an escalating military conflict, with the U.S. military striking a broader range of targets in Iran and Iran launching attacks across the Persian Gulf. The clash cente...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla Orders Reevaluation of 2019 SEA Games Sports Facilities Case
item.description: The Ombudsman's Office has initiated a new review of the 2019 Southeast Asian Games (SEA Games) sports facilities construction case, which was previously dismissed due to insufficient evidence. Ombuds...
